japan.title: Ultimate Japan Student Visa & Scholarship Guide 2026 for Bangladeshi Students
japan.description: A complete roadmap for Bangladeshi students seeking higher education in Japan. Covering the COE process, MEXT and JASSO scholarship details, financial solvency requirements, and 2026 approval trends.

visa_category_details.entry_type: Single Entry (Converted to Residence Card upon arrival)
visa_category_details.eligibility: Applicants with an Admission Offer and an issued Certificate of Eligibility (COE).

government_funded.name: MEXT (Monbukagakusho) Scholarship
government_funded.benefits: Full tuition waiver, monthly stipend (¥117,000 - ¥145,000), and round-trip airfare.
government_funded.application_window: April–May (Embassy Recommendation) or via University Recommendation.
government_funded.selection_criteria: High academic GPA, research proposal (for postgrads), and written exams.

private_and_partial.name: JASSO Reservation Program
private_and_partial.benefits: Monthly stipend of ¥48,000 for EJU high-scorers.
private_and_partial.target: Self-funded students with excellent entrance results.

private_and_partial.name: Honjo International Scholarship Foundation
private_and_partial.benefits: Monthly stipend up to ¥210,000 for graduate students.
private_and_partial.special_note: Requires proficiency in Japanese or high-level English research.

financial_solvency_thresholds.self_funded_min_balance: BDT 2,500,000 - 3,500,000 (Equivalent to 1st year tuition + ¥2,000,000 living costs)
financial_solvency_thresholds.sponsor_profile: First-degree relative (Father/Mother) preferred with stable monthly income.
financial_solvency_thresholds.bank_history_requirement: 6 to 12 months of consistent transaction history; lump-sum deposits are highly scrutinized.
financial_solvency_thresholds.logic: The Japanese Immigration Bureau requires proof that the student can survive without illegal work.

embassy_submission_docs: Original Passport (Valid for duration of stay)
Visa Application Form (Typed with QR Code)
Acceptance Letter from the Japanese Institution
Educational Certificates & Transcripts (Attested by Ministry of Education/Foreign Affairs)
coe_phase_documents: Sponsor's Income Tax Return (ITDR) for last 3 years
Relationship proof (Birth Certificate or Family Tree)
Language Proficiency Proof (JLPT N5+ or NAT-Test for language students; IELTS/TOEFL for English programs)

processing_and_approval_metrics.coe_processing_time: 2 - 3 months (Issued by regional Immigration Bureaus in Japan)
processing_and_approval_metrics.visa_processing_time: 5 - 10 working days after COE submission at JVAC
processing_and_approval_metrics.approval_chances: High (90%+) once a COE is issued, provided documents are not forged.
processing_and_approval_metrics.interview_probability: Medium. Students may be called for a basic interview to verify language skills or study intent.

high_risk_reasons: Inconsistency between COE documents and Embassy visa application.
Lack of Japanese language ability (inability to answer basic questions in Japanese).
Poor academic record without a valid explanation for study gaps.
rejection_risk_matrix.mitigation_strategy: Ensure all translations (Bengali to English/Japanese) are notarized and match the original data exactly.

work_rights_and_stay.part_time_permission: Up to 28 hours per week (requires 'Permission to Engage in Activity other than that Permitted' at the airport).
work_rights_and_stay.vacation_hours: Up to 40 hours per week during long academic breaks.
work_rights_and_stay.visa_extension: Renewable yearly or every 2 years based on enrollment status.

faq_student_edition.question: Can I apply for a student visa without a JLPT certificate?
faq_student_edition.answer: For English-medium degree programs, IELTS/TOEFL is sufficient. For language schools, at least 150 hours of Japanese study or an N5 certificate is usually mandatory for COE approval.

faq_student_edition.question: Is there an age limit for Japanese student visas?
faq_student_edition.answer: Technically no, but those over 30 or with a study gap of 5+ years face much stricter scrutiny regarding their motivation.

faq_student_edition.question: Can my spouse join me?
faq_student_edition.answer: Only after you arrive and switch to a Dependent Visa, which usually requires showing a higher bank balance and stable living conditions.

canada.title: Comprehensive Canada Study Permit & Scholarship Guide 2026 for Bangladeshi Students
canada.description: The definitive 2026 roadmap for Bangladeshi students pursuing higher education in Canada. Covering the PAL process, SDS vs. Non-SDS streams, GIC requirements, and updated cost-of-living thresholds.

visa_category_details.entry_type: TRV (Temporary Resident Visa) or eTA (Electronic Travel Authorization)
visa_category_details.eligibility: Applicants with a Letter of Acceptance (LOA) and a Provincial Attestation Letter (PAL) from a DLI.

government_funded.name: Vanier Canada Graduate Scholarships
government_funded.benefits: $50,000 per year for three years for doctoral students.
government_funded.application_window: Typically closes in October/November for the following academic year.
government_funded.selection_criteria: Academic excellence, research potential, and demonstrated leadership skills.

private_and_partial.name: Lester B. Pearson International Scholarship
private_and_partial.benefits: Full tuition, books, incidental fees, and full residence support for four years.
private_and_partial.target: Exceptional undergraduate students at the University of Toronto.

private_and_partial.name: Canada ASEAN Scholarships and Educational Exchanges for Development (SEED)
private_and_partial.benefits: Funding for short-term study or research for students from ASEAN/Specific regions.
private_and_partial.special_note: Focused on mid-career professionals and students in specific sustainable development fields.

financial_solvency_thresholds.self_funded_min_balance: BDT 3,500,000 - 5,000,000 (1st year tuition + $20,635 CAD GIC/Living + Travel)
financial_solvency_thresholds.sponsor_profile: Immediate family (Parents) with clear Proof of Funds and 'Source of Wealth' documentation.
financial_solvency_thresholds.bank_history_requirement: Consistent 6-month history; large unexplained deposits lead to immediate 'Personal Assets' concerns.
financial_solvency_thresholds.logic: IRCC requires proof that students can support themselves without depending on off-campus employment.

embassy_submission_docs: Valid Passport (Minimum 6 months validity beyond intended stay)
Letter of Acceptance (LOA) from a Designated Learning Institution (DLI)
Provincial Attestation Letter (PAL) - Mandatory for most 2026 undergrad applications
Recent Digital Photo (35mm x 45mm, White background)
Educational Credentials (WES evaluation highly recommended for Master's)
coe_phase_documents: Study Plan / Statement of Purpose (SOP) explaining why Canada and why this specific program
Guaranteed Investment Certificate (GIC) of $20,635 CAD (via Scotiabank/CIBC)
Proof of Tuition Payment for the first year
CAQ (For students intending to study in Quebec)
Language Proficiency (IELTS Academic 6.0-6.5+ or PTE Academic for SDS eligibility)

processing_and_approval_metrics.coe_processing_time: 8 - 12 weeks (Global average for Bangladesh via the Non-SDS or SDS stream)
processing_and_approval_metrics.visa_processing_time: 20 - 45 days after Biometrics and Medical completion
processing_and_approval_metrics.approval_chances: Variable (65% - 85%); higher for Master’s/PhD and those with strong GIC/SOP documentation.
processing_and_approval_metrics.interview_probability: Low. Decisions are primarily document-based; medical exam is mandatory.

high_risk_reasons: Dual Intent/Vague ties to home country (Section 216(1) concerns).
Weak Statement of Purpose (SOP) that doesn't justify the career progression.
Insufficient financial proof or lack of a GIC (for high-risk profiles).
Mismatch between previous education and the proposed program in Canada.
rejection_risk_matrix.mitigation_strategy: Submit a robust 'Property & Family Ties' evaluation and clear 'Tax Returns' for sponsors.

work_rights_and_stay.part_time_permission: Up to 24 hours per week off-campus (2026 policy) during academic sessions.
work_rights_and_stay.vacation_hours: Full-time hours permitted during scheduled summer or winter breaks.
work_rights_and_stay.visa_extension: Eligible for Post-Graduation Work Permit (PGWP) of up to 3 years depending on program length.

faq_student_edition.question: Can I apply for a Canada student visa with PTE?
faq_student_edition.answer: Yes, IRCC accepts PTE Academic for the SDS stream (minimum 60 score) and regular applications.

faq_student_edition.question: What is a PAL and do I need it in 2026?
faq_student_edition.answer: A Provincial Attestation Letter is mandatory for most undergraduate and college students to prove they fall under the provincial cap.

faq_student_edition.question: Can I bring my family while I study?
faq_student_edition.answer: Currently, only spouses of students in Master's/Doctoral or professional programs are eligible for an Open Work Permit.
